sourceList.title: Thomas Gardner in entry for Rebecka Gardner, "England Births and Christenings, 1538-1975"
Alice Gardner.  Parents Thomas and Elizabeth Gardner. Thomas is shown as a shoemaker.  This couples marriage in England & Wales Registers, and marriage Index.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Gardner於1529年出生在英國素福爾可斯普盧頓時，他的父親William Gardner25歲，母親Lucy Francis17歲。他於1557年8月與Agnes Keddington在英國英格蘭素福爾可格萊姆斯福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1603年3月19日在他的家鄉享年74歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可斯普盧頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Gardner于1529年出生在英国素福爾可斯普盧頓时，他的父亲William Gardner25岁，母亲Lucy Francis17岁。他于1557年8月与Agnes Keddington在英国英格兰素福爾可格萊姆斯福德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1603年3月19日在他的家乡享年74岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可斯普盧頓。

nameMeanings.name: Gardner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name from Middle English gardener ‘gardener’, Old French gardinier, jardinier.
Americanized form (translation into English) of German Gärtner or Gartner and French Desjardins .

variants.name: Garden
meanings: English and Scottish: metonymic occupational name for a gardener or topographic name for one who lived by a garden, from Middle English, Older Scots gardin, garde(i)n ‘garden; enclosed area for ground for cultivation’, Old French gardin. Compare Gardner .
German (North Rhine-Westphalia): patronymic from   Gard   or   Garde  .
French: occupational name for a guardian, from gardenc, an old derivative of Old French garde ‘watch, protection’. This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Gartner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Gerda, Johann, Lorenz, Otto, Wolfgang, Bernd, Dieter, Frieda, Fritz, Gerhard.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gärtner): occupational name for a gardener or vintner, from an agent derivative of Middle High German garte ‘enclosure, garden’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and in Slovenia (see 2 below). Compare Gaertner and Gardner .
Slovenian: of German origin (see 1 above). In North America, this surname may also be an altered form of the Slovenized variant Gartnar.

variants.name: Gardiner
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Gardner  .

History: Lion Gardiner came from England in 1635 to Saybrook, CT, the settlement of Earl of Warwick patentees at the mouth of the Connecticut River, and built a fort there. Born in 1636, his son, David, was the first white child born in the settlement. Lion later bought the Isle of Wight, now Gardiners Island, from the Indians, and moved his family there until 1653, when he bought land in what is now Easthampton, Long Island, NY.

variants.name: Giardino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Cosmo, Nicola, Rocco, Romeo, Sal, Vito.

Italian (mainly south): from giardino ‘garden’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a gardener (see Gardner ), a topographic name for someone who lived by a garden, or a habitational name from any of various places called Giardino, for example in Bologna, or from Giardina Gallotti, or Giardinello in Palermo.

variants.name: Cartner
meanings: English (Cumberland): variant of   Gardner   in which /g/ and /d/ have been devoiced.
Americanized form of German Gärtner ‘gardener’ (see Gartner , compare 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Gardner was born in 1529, in Sproughton, Suffolk, England, his father, William Gardner, was 25 and his mother, Lucy Francis, was 17. He married Agnes Keddington in August 1557, in Glemsford, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 19 March 1603, in his hometown, at the age of 74, and was buried in Sproughton, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Gardner est né en 1529, à Sproughton, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Gardner, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lucy Francis, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Keddington en août 1557, à Glemsford, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 19 mars 1603, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Sproughton, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Gardner 1529 in Sproughton, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Gardner 25 und seine Mutter Lucy Francis 17. Er hat im August 1557 in Glemsford, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Agnes Keddington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 19. März 1603 im Alter von 74 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Sproughton, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Gardner è nato il nel 1529 nel Sproughton, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, William Gardner, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lucy Francis, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Keddington nel agosto 1557, nel Glemsford, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 19 marzo 1603, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Sproughton, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Gardnerが1529年にイギリススフォルクスプロウトンで生まれた時，父William Gardnerは25歳，母Lucy Francisは17歳であった。1557年8月にイギリスイギリススフォルクグレムスフォードでAgnes Keddingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1603年3月19日に故郷で74歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクスプロウトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Gardner(이)가 1529년 영국 수폴크 스프루그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Gardner(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucy Francis(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1557년 8월 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 글레스퍼드에서 Agnes Keddington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1603년 3월 19일 그의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 스프루그h턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Gardner nasceu em 1529, em Sproughton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Gardner, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lucy Francis, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Keddington em agosto de 1557, em Glemsford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de março de 1603, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Sproughton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Gardner родился в 1529 г. в местности спроугтон, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Gardner было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucy Francis, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Keddington август 1557 г. в местности глемсфорд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 19 марта 1603 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности спроугтон, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Gardner nació en 1529, en Sproughton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Gardner, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lucy Francis, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Agnes Keddington en agosto de 1557, en Glemsford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 19 de marzo de 1603, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Sproughton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
